title: Build a wheel module
description: Assemble a single drive pod: drive motor, gearbox, and 4" traction wheel. Build six of these in total — three per side.
alt: Close-up of a single drive motor, gearbox, and 4-inch traction wheel subassembly

title: Drive pod reference drawing
description: Use this drawing for hardware callouts, fastener sizes, and orientation detail while assembling each drive pod.
alt: Engineering drawing of the drive pod subassembly

title: Inspect wheel and axle hardware
description: Side view detail of the wheel hub, bearing block, and through-shaft. Confirm the bearing seats fully and the shaft is retained on both sides before moving on.
alt: Side view detail of a traction wheel, bearing block, and axle shaft

title: Mount three wheel modules into the side rail
description: Drop three completed wheel modules into one perforated side rail and fasten them through the rail. Repeat to produce a matching second side.
alt: Three traction wheels installed in one perforated side rail, front-on view

title: Bolt the drive motors to the side rail
description: Bolt two drive motors to the inside face of the same side rail and couple them to their wheels. Repeat steps 3–4 for the opposite rail before joining them.
alt: One drive side rail with three wheels and two drive motors fitted

title: Join both side rails into the wheelbase
description: Connect the two completed side rails with the cross-tubes to form the six-wheel drivetrain. Check that the frame is square before tightening fasteners.
alt: Two drive side rails joined by cross-tubes forming the complete drivetrain

title: Mount the belly-pan / electronics plate
description: Drop the central electronics-mount plate onto the drivetrain and fasten it to the cross-tubes. This plate carries the battery, PDH, and motor controllers.
alt: Drivetrain with the central electronics-mount belly pan installed

title: Verify belly-pan alignment (side view)
description: Same assembly viewed from a different corner. Confirm the belly pan sits flat against every standoff with no gaps.
alt: Drivetrain with belly pan installed, alternate angle

title: Verify belly-pan alignment (front view)
description: Front-quarter view of the drivetrain and belly pan. Use this angle to check that nothing protrudes past the bumper line.
alt: Front-quarter view of the drivetrain with belly pan installed

title: Lay out the electronics on the belly pan
description: Top-down reference for placement of the PDH, breakers, motor controllers, roboRIO, and radio. Lay components out before drilling or fastening anything.
alt: Top-down view of the electronics layout on the belly pan

title: Install the side superstructure panels
description: Bolt the two side superstructure plates to the drive base. These panels carry the intake and dispenser hardware in later steps.
alt: Two side superstructure panels bolted to the drive base

title: Reference: side-panel exploded view
description: Exploded layout of the four superstructure plates showing their orientation and fastener pattern. Use this as a reference if a panel is installed backwards.
alt: Exploded view of the four superstructure side plates

title: Populate electronics and side panels
description: Side panels fitted with the electronics populated on the belly pan. Confirm wiring routes cleanly between the PDH and each motor controller.
alt: Side panels installed with electronics populated on the belly pan, angle A

title: Side-panel + electronics check (angle B)
description: Alternate angle of the same stage. Use this view to confirm fastener locations along the top edge of the side panels.
alt: Side panels and electronics, alternate angle B

title: Side-panel + electronics check (angle C)
description: Another angle of the same assembly stage to verify clearance around the motor controllers.
alt: Side panels and electronics, alternate angle C

title: Side-panel + electronics check (battery side)
description: View from the battery side showing the bay clear of obstructions. Confirm the battery can slide in and out without snagging on wiring.
alt: Side panels and electronics viewed from the battery bay side

title: Add the front intake rollers
description: Install the front intake roller assembly between the side panels. A game piece is shown in position to confirm the intake gap is correct.
alt: Front intake rollers installed with a game piece staged for reference

title: Install the upper intake / dispenser shaft
description: Fit the top dispenser shaft and its mounting plates above the intake. Make sure the shaft turns freely before mounting the upper guards.
alt: Upper intake and dispenser shaft installed above the front rollers

title: Assemble the full intake + dispenser
description: Complete intake and dispenser assembly seated on the drivetrain, shown without upper guards so the linkage is visible.
alt: Full intake and dispenser assembly on the drivetrain, upper guards removed

title: Fit the upper guards
description: Install the upper coral / note dispenser guards. The robot is now mechanically complete; only final inspection remains.
alt: Upper coral and note dispenser guards installed on the near-complete robot

title: Final assembly check
description: Fully assembled robot, hero view. Walk around the robot and confirm every subsystem is fastened, wired, and clear of moving parts.
alt: Fully assembled WA Robotics 2026 kit robot, hero view
